column1: I'll take it, you blurt. "Say, if you have no seats available, why were all those men in line?"
column2: 「我要了，」你脫口而出。「話說，如果沒有空位，那排隊的那些男人是在排什麼？」

column1: They were waiting for the keys to the restroom!
You presume that explains their sudden departure following the loud noise!
column2: 你猜這就是為什麼一聲巨響後，他們全都匆匆離開的原因！

column1: Would you prefer smoking or non-smoking?
Non.
Aisle or window?
Aisle.
Any carry-on baggage?
None.
Luggage to check through?
No.
Will you require a bassinet?
Traveling with children or elderly?
Bringing a pet?
Do you have a passport or visa?
Passport.
Care for baggage insurance?
First class, business class or coach?
You said there was only one seat.
Will you be staying 14 to 21 days?
Forever.
Are you a member of any frequent flyer programs?
column2: 「有加入哪個飛行常客方案嗎？」

column1: Will you be bringing a laptop computer on-board?
Would you like a pillow?
Yes.
Blanket?
Movie?
Stereo headphones?
I suppose.
Drinking or non-drinking?
Drinking.
Cash or credit card?
Cash.
Telephone privileges in flight?
Kosher or Mexican?
Armenian!
3-1/2 inch or 5-1/4 inch?
8-bit or 16-bit?
Stewardess fondling or non-fondling?
JUST GIVE ME THE DAMN TICKET!!
JUST GIVE ME THE GOL-DARNED, DAB-NATTED, GOLLY-GEE-WHIZ-BANGED TICKET!!
column2: 「給我那張夭壽的、天殺的、有夠靠背的機票就對了啦！！」

column1: Boy, are you Americanos touchy! he snarls, "Ok, here's your ticket: one adult, ultra-saver, non-smoking, window, Deluxe-Business Class, Express, APEX, excursion, Gold Label, y-fare, non-refundable, maxifare ticket for Calwa, with pillow, blanket, movie, and stereo headphones."
column2: 「哎唷，你們美國佬脾氣真差！」他咆哮道，「好啦，這是你的機票：一張成人、超級省錢、非吸菸、靠窗、豪華商務艙、快速、APEX優惠、旅遊票、金牌、Y艙等、不可退票、最高票價的卡爾瓦機票，附贈枕頭、毯子、電影和立體聲耳機。」

column1: Your flight is scheduled to depart from Gate #1 in exactly one minute.
column2: 「您的班機預定將於一分鐘後準時從1號登機門起飛。」

column1: Too bad you spent so much time here; you might have made it! concludes the agent, "%s"
column2: 「真可惜你在這裡耗了那麼多時間，不然你或許還趕得上！」地勤人員下了結論，「%s」

column1: You'd better not let him see that!
column2: 你最好別讓他看到那個！

column1: That won't help you with this guy.
Here ya go, buddy, you tell him, "but remember: this picture was taken before my recent hair rejuvenation."
column2: 「拿去吧，老兄，」你對他說，「不過別忘了，這張照片是我最近做完生髮療程之前拍的。」

column1: I'm sorry, Senor, he responds, "but this is not your passport. I'm afraid I cannot allow you to pass this point."
column2: 「抱歉，先生，」他回答，「但這不是你的護照。恐怕我不能讓你通過這裡。」

column1: You again?! Did you get rid of that knife?
column2: 「又是你？！你把那把刀處理掉了沒？」

column1: Of course, would I lie to you? you say unabashed.
column2: 「當然啊，我會騙你嗎？」你厚著臉皮說。

column1: Ok, I've seen enough of your stuff! Go on through, but please remember: %s
column2: 「好啦，你的東西我看夠了！過去吧，但請記住：%s」

column1: You again?! I've seen enough of your stuff! Just go on through; and remember: %s
column2: 「又是你？！你的東西我看夠了！直接過去吧，記住：%s」

column1: A gentleman with a pleasant appearance and friendly smile waits by the only open Customs Inspection counter.
column2: 一位外表和善、笑容親切的先生站在唯一開放的海關檢查櫃檯旁等著。

column1: Those damned dancin' raisins are EVERYWHERE!
column2: 那些該死的跳舞葡萄乾到處都是！

column1: The conveyor belts disappear through the wall to the next room, where they are thoroughly inspected by sharp-eyed Customs Agents.
column2: 輸送帶穿過牆壁延伸到隔壁房間，那裡有眼尖的海關人員仔細檢查。

column1: For such a small airport, they certainly have an impressive Customs Inspection station.
column2: 以這麼小的機場來說，他們的海關檢查站還真是氣派。

column1: There is a barely perceptible gate in the far wall.
column2: 遠處牆上有一道幾乎看不出來的門。

column1: You can't. The conveyor belts are for luggage only!
column2: 不行，輸送帶只給行李用！

column1: The rope is firmly affixed, and won't help you get by here.
column2: 這條繩子被牢牢固定住，沒辦法幫你從這裡過去。

column1: You can't. It's controlled by the Customs Agent behind the counter.
column2: 不行，這是由櫃檯後的海關人員控制的。

column1: Good day, Senor, you say to the man. %s
Good day, Sir, he replies in like fashion. "May I please see your passport?"
column2: 「日安，先生，」他也同樣回禮。「可以讓我看一下你的護照嗎？」

column1: You hand the agent your passport.
column2: 你把護照遞給海關人員。

column1: This picture is an excellent likeness, says the Customs Agent. "It must be printed on photographic paper by Kodork!"
column2: 「這張照片拍得真傳神，」海關人員說，「一定是用柯多克相紙印出來的！」

column1: Now, I must inspect your possessions, he says. "Please show me everything you are carrying."
column2: 「現在，我得檢查你身上的東西，」他說，「請把你帶的所有東西都拿出來給我看。」

column1: Well, ok, I guess, you reply, "although this is highly unusual in an adventure game..."
column2: 「呃，好吧，我想可以，」你回答，「雖然這在冒險遊戲裡挺不尋常的……」

column1: There. Are you satisfied? you ask.
Yes, but I must warn you about carrying that knife on board any aircraft.
column2: 「滿意，但我得警告你，不能帶那把刀上任何飛機。」

column1: Ok, you lie. "You have my word on it!"
All right, he says, unlocking the gate in the east wall, "you may pass."
column2: 「好吧，」他一邊說，一邊打開東牆的門，「你可以過去了。」

column1: Hey, thanks a lot, buddy, you say, peeling a hundred off your wad of bills, "and here's a little something for you and the missus!"
column2: 「嘿，太謝謝你了，老兄，」你邊說邊從一疊鈔票裡抽出一張百元大鈔，「這是給你跟你老婆的一點小意思！」

column1: I'm not your buddy, you ugly Americano. %s
column2: 「我才不是你老兄，你這醜陋的美國佬。%s」

column1: LOOK OUT, I'VE GOT A BOMB!!! you shout.
Of course, the agent casually replies, "%s"
column2: 海關人員一派輕鬆地回答，「%s」

column1: Luggage passes by on the nearby conveyor belt.
column2: 附近的輸送帶上正有行李經過。

column1: What a pair of lips!
Conveyors carrying luggage stretch off into the distance.
column2: 載著行李的輸送帶一路延伸到遠方。

column1: He's sound asleep. Shhh! Don't wake him.
column2: 他睡得正熟。噓！別吵醒他。

column1: Gawd, is that symbolic, or what?!
column2: 天啊，這也太有象徵意義了吧？！

column1: (You fondly recall an old joke about a girl and her cherry.)
column2: （你想起了一個關於女孩和她的櫻桃的老笑話，會心一笑。）

column1: A security guard snoozes beside his X-ray machine, allowing baggage to pass by uninspected.
column2: 一名警衛在X光機旁打瞌睡，讓行李未經檢查就通過了。

column1: Zzzzzzz.
You missed!
You nimbly grab someone's suitcase from the moving belt, force it open, and discover inside...
column2: 你敏捷地從移動的輸送帶上抓起某人的行李箱，強行打開，發現裡面……

column1: If you play this game backwards, you can read: "Paul is dead."
column2: 如果你把這個遊戲倒過來玩，你會看到：「保羅已死。」

column1: ...a disassembled hunting rifle. You decide it is of no use to you.
column2: ……一把拆解開的獵槍。你覺得這對你沒用。

column1: ...some dirty underwear.
...a dead cat.
YUCK!
...a pair of maces. You decide they are of no use to you.
column2: ……一對狼牙棒。你覺得這對你沒用。

column1: ...a Tommy gun in a violin case. You decide it is of no use to you.
column2: ……藏在小提琴盒裡的湯普森衝鋒槍。你覺得這對你沒用。

column1: ...nothing of interest.
...a computer. Searching through the disk storage box within, you discover a complete set of Sierra adventure games. You decide they are of no use to you.
column2: ……一台電腦。翻找裡頭的磁片收納盒，你發現一整套Sierra冒險遊戲。你覺得這對你沒用。

column1: ...because you're having plenty of trouble with THIS game!
column2: ……因為你光是這款遊戲就搞得焦頭爛額了！

column1: John 3:16
...some blue, pin-striped suits. You decide they are of no use to you.
column2: ……幾套藍色細條紋西裝。你覺得這對你沒用。

column1: ...a fully-automatic machine gun and some clips of ammo. You realize you have no use for this until you purchase "Police Quest II, The Vengeance."
column2: ……一把全自動機關槍和幾個彈匣。你發現在買到《警察任務II：復仇》之前，這東西對你沒用。

column1: You sheepishly close it, return it to the conveyor, and wonder if anyone noticed you.
column2: 你心虛地把它闔上，放回輸送帶上，心想有沒有人注意到你。

column1: A BOMB!!
Let's see now, you think, "I'm holding a suitcase containing a ticking bomb. What shall I do?"
column2: 「讓我想想，」你心想，「我手上拿著一個裝有定時炸彈的行李箱，我該怎麼辦？」
